contracts.label: Mountain recipe
contracts.notes: Mountain is the first audited Gaea Art recipe and is the main PE reuse source.

contracts.label: Canyon bridge oracle
contracts.notes: Reusable invoke-static oracle pattern for generator nodes with multi-output raw buffers.

contracts.label: Canyon recipe
contracts.notes: Focused closed for default and small non-default probes; wider sweeps still gate full certification.

contracts.label: PE trace bilinear anchor
contracts.notes: Shared Parallel Erosion trace sampling contract.

contracts.label: PE commit capacity with extra
contracts.notes: Shared Parallel Erosion carrying-capacity contract.

contracts.label: Voronoi raw substrate
contracts.notes: Reusable raw Voronoi/noise substrate.

contracts.label: FractalWarp virtual identity sampling
contracts.notes: Virtual warp samples identity vector field first; direct scalar bilinear shortcuts are not bit-equivalent after PE amplification.

contracts.label: Combiner subtract ratio mix
contracts.notes: Gaea subtract ratio is mix(lhs, lhs - rhs, ratio), not lhs - rhs * ratio.

contracts.label: Fractal Terrace height path
contracts.notes: Live path computes height only; debug path can still capture full internals.

contracts.label: FractalTerraces node runtime
contracts.notes: Focused raw Bridge/native closure for HeightField and Layers. Style C primary warp is VoronoiA via Gaea nodes int constant key 2 -> enum value 3.

contracts.label: Sea Global no-coastal runtime
contracts.notes: Scoped raw Bridge/native closure for Global/no-coastal height, sea, depth, shore, and surface outputs. Latest Sea full-promotion gate is 24/24 exact.

contracts.label: Sea Surrounding no-coastal runtime
contracts.notes: Scoped raw Bridge/native closure for Surrounding/no-coastal EdgeFill behavior. Latest Sea full-promotion gate is 24/24 exact.

contracts.label: Sea Global coastal runtime
contracts.notes: Scoped raw Bridge/native closure for Global/coastal shoreface, BGBlurI64, DistanceTransform, and cliff-noise behavior.

contracts.label: Sea Surrounding coastal runtime
contracts.notes: Scoped raw Bridge/native closure for Surrounding/coastal EdgeFill and shoreface behavior, including closed-basin no-mask rejection.

contracts.label: Sea node runtime
contracts.notes: Full current Sea branch matrix is exact: Global/no-coastal, Surrounding/no-coastal, Global/coastal, and Surrounding/coastal are all covered by a 24/24 epsilon-0 raw buffer promotion gate.

contracts.label: Snow runtime frontier
contracts.notes: Focused raw Bridge/native frontier for HeightField, Snow, SoftMask, and HardMask. Latest strict devflywheeltool focused matrix D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\snow-bridge-probe\matrix_1779894898087531100_65476_0\matrix_report.json exits 0 under --require-all-pass --require-exact and is 4/4 exact at epsilon 0 with HeightField/Snow/SoftMask/HardMask exact_count=4/4, worst_max_abs_delta=0, and worst_max_ulp_delta=0. Closure depends on native Erosion1 interleaved tick row order plus LAB_180002170 final writeback order. Keep this focused_closed until wider parameter and Snowfield cascade re-hit coverage are promoted.

contracts.label: Snowfield runtime frontier
contracts.notes: Focused no-GroundTexture Snowfield closure: the built-in focused matrix covers cone8 and cone16 and the strict devflywheeltool wrapper artifact D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\snowfield-bridge-probe\matrix_1779891529156455100_69480_0\matrix_report.json reports 2/2 exact at epsilon 0, with Height/Depth/Snow/Hard exact_count=2/2 and worst_max_abs_delta=0. Closure depends on Snow focused closure, Adjustments.Blur amount-to-radius divisor 40, Profile.FastBlur4 radius-scale preservation, and removing the non-Gaea ULP snap. Keep SurfaceDetails/GroundTexture and Flows outside this closure until their own raw gates pass.

contracts.label: Denoise GraphicEQ modes
contracts.notes: Scoped raw Bridge/native closure for OnePass and TwoPass GraphicEQ-backed modes. Pixels/HardDenoise remains excluded because the bridge path exposes order-dependent in-place mutation.

contracts.label: Gabor node runtime
contracts.notes: Raw Bridge/native closure for the shared Gabor scalar and anisotropy-modulated path. DuneSea still needs downstream recipe closure.

contracts.label: Distance node runtime
contracts.notes: Shared Distance/Morphology substrate covering Morphology.DistanceTransform and Morph.DistanceRT focused raw parity. Downstream Plates and HydroFix series closure remains the next gate.

contracts.label: Lake node runtime
contracts.notes: Flat Lake case is exact and Surface is exact across the focused matrix. Latest product-path strict diagnostic matrix D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\lake-bridge-probe\matrix_1779895569814218000_65860_0\matrix_report.json preserves 1/7 exact and water 5/7 exact, adds native_preview_model diagnostics for provisional level, retained volume ratio, target volume, final level, and retention loss terms, and splits blockers into f32_tail_only=1, level_retention=3, water_mask_topology=1, and zero_depth_water_topology=1. The new model diagnostics show native retained_volume_ratio overshoots the Bridge-required ratio for basin16 by 0.000194790759257768 and ramp16 by about 0.00157-0.00159, while cone16 is the opposite sign at 0.000020400242698468. RampX/RampY also have slightly different Bridge target levels despite the symmetric native model, so scalar coefficient tuning is not a valid closure path. Direct ErosionWrapperW --fixed-threads false and true have identical lower-level summaries with 2 f32_tail_only and 2 level_retention cases, so thread count is not the active blocker. Full runtime remains open on retained-level/writeback ordering, fragmented boundary water masks, near-zero edge water-mask behavior, and shore propagation.

contracts.label: Linear gradient triangle domain
contracts.notes: Gradient domain, wrapping, and direction constants are reusable across art recipes.

contracts.label: Normalize/finalize minimum-axis adjustment
contracts.notes: Shared normalize/invert/finalize tail semantics for generator outputs.

contracts.label: Scalar-first map and port contract
contracts.notes: Captures scalar height carrier, inherited primary input, auxiliary map outputs, and explicit connection-point counts.

contracts.label: Erosions.PE public shell
contracts.notes: Public PE wrapper parameter order and output slot remap: height, flow, wear, deposition.

contracts.label: EasyErosion QuickErosion recipe stack
contracts.notes: Thin recipe shell: bias overlay, QuickErosion branch selection, PE preset stack, and save-slot wiring.

contracts.label: Combiner min/max height shell
contracts.notes: Shared `Combiner.Min` and `Combiner.Max` height composition used by EasyErosion exposed/flows/strata branches.

contracts.label: Linear gradient bias overlay
contracts.notes: EasyErosion optional bias uses add-before/subtract-after height-only overlay; generic `Gradients.LinearGradient` still needs closure.

contracts.label: RockCore shared substrate
contracts.notes: RockCore now has a strict static rawf32 oracle compare gate over Large, Noise, and Outcrops plus three bottom-layer Large Perlin stage oracles from the real Gaea harness. The latest matrix remains open at 3/8 exact: Large.Perlin is 3/3 bit-exact, but Large final still first-mismatches at sample 0, Noise is 0/1 exact, Outcrops is 0/3 exact with cone/node-default drift up to 0.12241623, and the flat16 Outcrops oracle is all NaN with 256 nonfinite mismatches while native currently sanitizes to finite values. This narrows RockCore.Large below Perlin and before promotion to BGBlurI64/FractalWarp/Voronoi/Recurve/final composition, while MultiTransform placement/filtering, SlopeBlur/Crumble, and Gaea nonfinite behavior remain open for dependent nodes.

contracts.label: Rock series focused raw parity
contracts.notes: Focused Rock series raw parity covers RockNoise Raw/Blur/Warp, Rockscape default Off/Off, Craggy default required-input flow, Rugged A-E, synthetic cone inputs, and Bridge Mountain connected upstream. It deliberately does not promote generic RockCore.Large/Outcrops, Rockscape cluster/relief sweeps, or Scree/Slump/Outcrops full runtime.

contracts.label: Slump node runtime
contracts.notes: Raw Bridge/native closure for Slump Scale/Style/Seed surface, Style A stage buffers, B/C/D Rugged final buffers, optional input multiply path in the Rust node runtime, and the production 20x speed gate. Focused matrix passes 7/7 exact at epsilon 0; production matrix passes 11/11 exact at epsilon 0 with min speedup 53.39189695442037x against the Bridge process.

contracts.label: RockCore noise overlay
contracts.notes: Used by EasyErosion Rocky branch; exact callsite arguments are known, but the reusable RockCore substrate remains open.

contracts.label: Classic erosion wrapper
next_commands: /gaea erosion-classic-substrate-compare --node Erosion --resolution 8 --source cone --max-steps 16 --volume 0.75 --debris 0.45 --area-effect ErosionStrength --area-mask map:checker:8:0.2:0.9:2 --sediment-removal 0.2 --sediment-removal-mask map:rampx:8:0.1:0.8 --direct-bin --run --json
contracts.notes: `Erosion` uses legacy `ErosionWrapper`, not the PE path. The focused Classic wrapper is exact under the decoded slot contract: slot0 height, slot1 wear, slot2 flow, slot3 deposits. The current probe coverage includes no-mask, explicit area-mask, explicit sediment-removal-mask, no-external-area-mask bias parameter paths, and non-default volume/debris paths. Volume/debris closure required matching native 44c0 cleanup between active 60f0 iterations and the lagged flow_aux marker coordinate contract. Full node promotion still depends on broader parameter/randomized sweeps, non-square, GPU, and desktop-app performance coverage.

contracts.label: Erosion area and sediment masks
contracts.notes: External area-mask, sediment-removal-mask, no-external-area-mask altitude/slope bias parameter paths, and masked volume/debris paths have focused Bridge/native exact evidence. Wider randomized mask/effect parameter sweeps remain outside this focused closure.

contracts.label: Erosion2 inhibitor and rain-shadow mask
contracts.notes: Erosion2 PE input mask is built from slope, normalized height, optional external mask, optional directional rain-shadow lighting, and reverse.

contracts.label: FlowMap node runtime
contracts.notes: FlowMap bridge/native focused matrix D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\flow-map-bridge-probe\matrix_1779895999501\matrix_report.json remains open at 1/4 exact and the strict gate exits 1 with --require-all-pass --require-exact. Devflywheeltool preserved the failed child stdout in D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\flow-map-bridge-probe\1779895986490\command_0_stdout.json while returning nonzero. The probe buckets boundary/interior and bridge-only/native-only residuals; latest evidence still points at real FlowSimulation routing/normalization semantics, not wrapper ABI, f32 tail, or output-amplitude tuning.

contracts.label: Crater New style Bridge/native acceptance
contracts.notes: Strict scoped New-style gate: 128x128 sweep seed 177984 passes 8/8 exact with audit_scope=focused and promotion_scope=crater.new_bridge_native_acceptance. Classic style and optional-input composition remain outside this acceptance.

contracts.label: Stones node runtime
contracts.notes: Connected-node runtime gate: focused matrix passes 6/6 exact across HeightField and Stones outputs, with audit_scope=node_runtime and promotion_scope=stones.node_runtime. Broader randomized sweeps, GPU-resident migration, and Gaea desktop app speed baseline are separate performance gates.

contracts.label: Weathering node runtime
contracts.notes: Weathering is opened as a native-preview runtime with the decompiled Soil2/equalize/shape/Screen base chain implemented. Base scalar explicit-weather closure is tracked separately at weathering.base_scalar_runtime. Current AO-only Bridge/native diagnostics keep Dirt AmbientOcclusion open: latest focused artifact D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\probe-bin\gaea_weathering_native_probe\1780512810191\command_0_stdout.json preserves raw residual mean_abs=0.004996341856895015/max_abs=0.031635344/rms_abs=0.00786705569871721 at q4/o2/normal_z=32/sky_z_min=-0.15, with managed Lighting2 and reconstructed NativeLighting.Parameters both proving AOModel=Hybrid, ShadowQuality=5, HybridAOQuality=4, HybridAOOctaves=2, and RaytracedAOQuality=4. Rust sphere diagnostics remain q4 with all_direction_count=2562, accepted_direction_count=1477, and accepted_direction_sha256_f32_xyz_le=56232540bfa40309933cb829f2ccab5be99e5d5175f2e9c157afc7d1c65f11ec, matching the native shade_ao_sphere_setup acceptance evidence of z > -0.15 and nonzero xy. The diagnostic helper now synchronizes a single bilinear option across base/entry/wrap sampling, restoring the production raw baseline; split-only bilinear candidates are kept as report-only localization evidence with best rank 67, while all-bilinear direct remains the best report-only raw candidate at rank 1 mean_abs=0.00183579002623446/max_abs=0.026271284. Rejected standalone fixes still include literal-key q5/o3, q3/o3 production, normal_z_scale=8, terminal-hit-drop, simple non-uniform ray_weight, direct uniform_half photon scaling, and native-offset wrap_reset_current_cell. Exact closure should continue below runtime q4/o2 and sphere acceptance/order in ShadeIspc trace-worker bilinear/index/entry/wrap semantics, then still cover missing-weather fallback, ColorTransport WashedOut branch, and RGB HSL support. Current-source ramp-x/ramp-y/sine AO artifacts reject native_clamp_fraction as a closure point and show sine best report-only improvement comes from scharr_clamp/octaves=1/direct bilinear, so AO closure must continue in native ShadeIspc normal/octave semantics rather than promoting report-only candidates. Fresh direct-bin AO artifacts 1780514823973/1780515158142/1780515524069 raise the focused report grid to policy_count=510 and keep speed gates true while rejecting native_clamp_fraction, global Scharr, and NativeGhidra root-detail as standalone closures; native evidence now proves Scharr 3/10/3 normals and terrain_width/resolution*32 z scaling, so the remaining AO closure should test root-normal versus lowest-layer photon-normal separation and layer reconstruction semantics. Split-normal AO diagnostics 1780516162273/1780516475418/1780516504206 show that root-vs-lowest normal separation is a real AO component: ramp-x/ramp-y still prefer the existing Sobel/direct/z22-z26 localization, but sine best raw moves to root Sobel plus lowest Scharr at q4/o2/z32/direct with mean_abs=0.016785434789198916 and max_abs=0.14170921. Reconstruct sampling is the next focused gate, but the current reconstruct_variant patch has not been runtime-verified because an unrelated RELAX/VEX scripting rebuild blocker stops the probe; see D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\probe-bin\gaea_weathering_native_probe\1780516831672\command_0_stderr.txt.

contracts.label: Weathering Lighting2 Hybrid AO reconstruct diagnostics
contracts.notes: Scoped evidence node for Weathering Lighting2 Hybrid AO reconstruct diagnostics. Artifact 1780518880119 verifies current-source rebuild after the RELAX/VEX blocker was cleared; reconstruct_variant best remains quarter_offset, matching native 9/3/3/1 reconstruct evidence. Direct and half-offset reconstruct rows are report-only rejects. Native reconstruct evidence also shows the core reconstruct kernel writes height_lf and AO, not normal_cos; for current q4/o2/default root detail, normal_cos writeback is not a live-output closure point.

contracts.label: Weathering Lighting2 SYCL lower-state diagnostics
contracts.notes: Scoped Weathering Lighting2 SYCL lower-state localization. Fresh AO-only artifacts 1780542053243/sine, 1780541890329/ramp-y, and 1780542745294/cone all report required_lower_delta_not_explained_by_exposed_lower_state with policy_count=889. Required-lower ray-policy scans do not close the residual: sine best mean improvement is 1.382431946694851e-9, ramp-y best mean improvement is 4.773028194904327e-9, and cone improvement is 0.0. The production downsample default is managed_cubic_4x4, and box/separable/downsample variants are rejected by the focused reports. Runtime metadata for the focused direct Lighting2 bridge predicts managed_sycl_functions_shade on CUDA GPU rather than static ShadeIspc. New F-backed harness diagnostics add manual SYCL replay with pre/post array dumps: sine64_oct1 and cone64_oct1 both compare managed_shade vs manual_sycl_replay exact with mismatch_count=0, mean_abs_delta=0, max_abs_delta=0, native_result=Success on RTX 5070 Ti CUDA. Flat64 oct1 and oct2 both produce constant AO SHA 51f7c927265a92ef951f39116ed3891ef2984fdd685a35f37c1e60f81434c369 and manual_sycl_replay exact with mismatch_count=0; the constant 0.9975742101669312 aligns within a small photon-integer rounding window with the Rust q4 all-sphere denominator and positive-z contribution model. Fresh q4/o2 F-backed Weathering-shape replays show the actual two-octave non-flat Lighting2 path also has exact managed/manual SYCL parity: sine64_oct2 AO SHA 25ba3dea811eb4016a0fec14ada3716ec611331ba200a06ec2aa9ec2c395a37c and cone64_oct2 AO SHA 0ec711e38e3feb266c59db3abb71fe74b173279295c25349794746042e63be2f, both mismatch_count=0. CPU lighting_root_reconstruct_row's height-detail nonpositive multiplier 1.0 is not promotable for the current CUDA SYCL Weathering path: native_ghidra_height_nonpositive_1p0 rows are rejected at sine rank 79 mean_abs=0.0033957545856537763/max_abs=0.028775275, ramp-y rank 80 mean_abs=0.000498862806125544/max_abs=0.010959387, and cone rank 114 mean_abs=0.0023294775310205296/max_abs=0.023295939. This closes managed wrapper/parameter glue, two-octave managed dispatch, CPU height-nonpositive root-detail promotion, and broad flat sphere/reconstruct drift as residual sources, pushing the next closure gate inside native SYCL Shade's non-flat lower AO and spectral layer state. A follow-up Rust source rebuild after updating report wording is blocked by unrelated RELAX/VEX compile errors in artifact 1780543002990; do not treat that blocker as Weathering AO evidence.

contracts.label: Weathering base scalar runtime
contracts.notes: Focused exact closure for the explicit-weather scalar branch only: Dirt=0, WashedOut=false, HSL controls zero. Fresh strict matrix passes 5/5 exact final height and actual Build stages with min native speedup 752.4472167862577x versus Bridge-stage elapsed time. Full Weathering remains open through weathering.node_runtime for Dirt/AO, ColorTransport, HSL, missing-weather fallback, and flat degenerate Bridge behavior.

nodes.label: Mountain
nodes.notes: Audited recipe and primary source of PE substrate closure.

nodes.label: Canyon
nodes.notes: Focused closed generator with depth output; good template for multi-output Gaea nodes.

nodes.label: Voronoi
nodes.notes: Reusable substrate node; its closure impacts several generators.

nodes.label: Ridge
nodes.notes: Ridge is audited_closed: default r16, 4-case r16 focused sweep, default r32, and a 20-case randomized resolution-128 sweep (1780423102625) all pass exact Bridge/native raw buffer parity at epsilon 0, covering Scale/Height/Definition/Seed/ScaleX/ScaleY branches. Remaining gates: source-input sweep (cone/radial/sine/rampx/checker), performance acceptance matrix entry with Gaea desktop-app cook baseline, and GPU-resident promotion.

nodes.label: Volcano
nodes.notes: Planned generator expected to reuse most Canyon/Mountain recipe primitives.

nodes.label: River
nodes.notes: Connected node; depends heavily on shared erosion and mask/combiner contracts.

nodes.label: Island
nodes.notes: Already has process probes; graph makes its reusable dependencies explicit.

nodes.label: CraterField
nodes.notes: Generator expected to benefit from closed raw noise and finalization contracts.

nodes.label: Crater
nodes.notes: New style Bridge/native flat-map acceptance is strict scoped-closed. Full Crater remains open because Classic style, Rim/Shape semantics, optional input composition, and ThermalShaper are not promoted by the New-style gate.

nodes.label: Stones
nodes.notes: Focused connected-node matrix is node_runtime closed for current Bridge/native raw buffers: 6/6 exact across HeightField and Stones outputs. Performance claim still needs a measured Gaea desktop baseline.

nodes.label: EasyErosion
nodes.notes: Connected one-input/four-output erosion recipe. The strict focused supported-subset gate now passes 9/9 exact at resolution 32 across Input, HeightField, Flow, Wear, and Deposits, with all_exact=true and worst_max_abs_delta=0 in probe-bin artifact 1780556397182. Current branch coverage includes Simple, Ancient, Alpine, Exposed, Flows, Flows2, Flows3, Rocky, Strata, Withered, SoftSoil, SoftSoil2, Dessicated, Thin, and BiasAngle X/N/E/S/W. Ancient2, broader randomized sweeps, non-square, GPU, and desktop-app performance coverage remain open.

nodes.label: Erosion2
nodes.notes: Recommended modern erosion node. PE substrate is bright; inhibitor/rain-shadow mask contract remains the open edge.

nodes.label: Erosion
nodes.notes: Legacy connected erosion node. It is not PE-backed; the focused Classic wrapper now has Bridge/native raw parity, decoded output labels, explicit external area/sediment mask exact cases, no-external-area-mask bias parameter exact cases, and focused non-default volume/debris exact cases. Remaining promotion gates are broader parameter/randomized sweeps, non-square, GPU, and desktop-app performance coverage.

nodes.label: Weathering
nodes.notes: High-frequency rock/weathering post-process. The explicit-weather scalar Soil2/equalize/shape/CLUT/Screen path is focused-closed by weathering.base_scalar_runtime. Full-node parity remains open for Dirt AO, Lighting2/ShadeIspc Hybrid AO trace-worker bilinear/index/entry/wrap semantics, ColorTransport, HSL, missing-weather fallback, flat degenerate Bridge behavior, and broader randomized sweeps. Current focused AO diagnostics promote only the native-evidenced AO ray acceptance filter z > -0.15 and q4/o2 runtime settings; latest artifact D:\ghost1.0\_c3d_devflywheeltool\probe-bin\gaea_weathering_native_probe\1780512810191\command_0_stdout.json keeps the production raw residual at mean_abs=0.004996341856895015/max_abs=0.031635344, records the Rust q4/o2 sphere/order fingerprint, and proves split-only bilinear candidates are report-only rather than promotable fixes. The best report-only ramp-x64 raw candidate remains wrap_then_bilinear_clamp_sky_z_gt_0_octaves_2_normal_z_26_bilinear_direct, so exact closure must continue below runtime q4/o2 and sphere acceptance/order in lower-level ShadeIspc trace-worker semantics. Fresh AO artifacts 1780513267086/1780513418161/1780513958884 update the open risk: native_clamp_fraction is report-only/rejected, and sine points to normal/octave semantics below Lighting2 q4/o2.
